items.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq Fall for 3rd Straight Day; Oil Prices Drop to Lowest Level Since Start of War
items.content: US markets fell for third consecutive session. S&P 500 dropped to 7,365. Nasdaq slid 2.21% to 25,587. Oil prices hit lowest since Iran war began on Hormuz reopening hopes.

items.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq fall as memory chip stocks tumble
items.content: S&P 500 fell 1.44% to 7,365.46. Tech-heavy Nasdaq slid 2.21% to 25,587.04 as memory chip-related shares tumbled on sector rotation.

items.title: S&P 500 at 7,366, Up 0.10%; Down 2.04% Over Past Month
items.content: US500 rose to 7,366 points on June 25, up 0.10% from previous session. Down 2.04% over past month but up 19.94% YoY.

items.title: Dow Ekes Out Gain as Big Tech Weighs on Nasdaq; SpaceX Falls to Lowest Close Ever
items.content: Dow managed small gain while Big Tech stocks dragged Nasdaq lower. SpaceX shares hit all-time low. Oil continued decline on US-Iran deal expectations.

items.title: Chinese Stocks in Hong Kong Enter Bear Market
items.content: Hang Seng China Enterprises Index fell 2%, entering bear market territory. Tepid consumer spending and fading confidence in e-commerce firms drove the decline.

items.title: Hang Seng Dips to 23,769 as Property Concerns Offset US-Iran Optimism
items.content: Hang Seng Index closed 0.65% lower at 23,769 on Monday, bucking positive Asia-Pacific trend. Property sector concerns weighed on sentiment.

items.title: Chinese Stocks in Hong Kong Near Bear Market After Holiday, Rebound on AI Names
items.content: Hang Seng China Enterprises Index narrowed decline to 0.3% from 2.3% intraday loss, rebounding on AI-related stock buying.

items.title: US Core PCE Hits 3.4% in May, In Line with Forecast
items.content: Core PCE inflation rose to 3.4% YoY in May, matching forecasts. Headline PCE accelerated to 4.1% from 3.8% in April, highest since late 2023, driven by energy prices.

items.title: US-Iran Memorandum: Hormuz Strait to Reopen, Blockade to End
items.content: Pakistan announced on June 18 that US-Iran MoU will reopen Strait of Hormuz and end US blockade of Iranian ports. Trump announced renewed ceasefire on June 19.

items.title: China Opens STAR Market Pathway for AI Model Firms Amid Funding Race
items.content: China Securities Regulatory Commission Chairman Wu Qing announced at Lujiazui Forum that STAR Market's fifth listing standard will open for AI model companies, aiming to boost AI funding.

items.title: No Noteworthy Earnings This Week; Focus on PCE Data
items.content: Earnings calendar June 22-26 light. No major earnings reports for the week. Market focus instead on May PCE inflation data released Thursday.

items.title: MKS Expands Guangzhou Plant for AI Build-Out
items.content: MKS Inc. (NASDAQ: MKSI) announced expansion of its Atotech equipment manufacturing site in Guangzhou, China, to support next wave of AI infrastructure build-out.
